description:
Analyze zoning non-conformance in San Francisco by identifying 'grandfathered' parcels that exceed current density limits using the Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/zoning_non_conformance.ipynb. Using the 'buildings', 'parcels', and 'zoning' tables from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5, calculate the built Floor Area Ratio (FAR) for all regulated parcels (where max_far > 0) and identify those where the built FAR exceeds the allowed maximum. Use a conversion of 43,560 square feet per acre for parcel area calculations.

Export the detailed list of non-conforming parcels to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/non_conforming_parcels.csv, including parcel_id, zone_id, total_building_sqft, parcel_sqft, built_far, max_far, and excess_sqft, sorted by excess_sqft in descending order. Save a summary of the number of grandfathered parcels per zone (sorted by count descending) to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/zone_non_conformance_counts.csv. Finally, produce a scatter plot visualizing built_far against max_far for all valid parcels, including a diagonal y=x reference line, and save it to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/far_compliance_scatter.png. Ensure all cells in the notebook are executed.
